title: Positive Test: MN Initiated Secondary Node Release Procedure Successful Completion
description: Verify that the MN can successfully initiate the SN Release procedure and the SN confirms the release, followed by proper UE context release and data forwarding.
preconditions: MN and SN are connected and UE is in EN-DC mode
SN context is established and data forwarding addresses are configured if applicable
testSteps: Access the 5G management interface and authenticate with appropriate credentials
From MN, send SgNB Release Request message to SN with data forwarding addresses if applicable
Verify SN receives request and sends SgNB Release Request Acknowledge confirming release
MN sends RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to UE indicating SCG configuration release
Verify UE applies SCG configuration release or performs reconfiguration failure procedure if unable
Verify SN sends SN Status Transfer message for RLC AM bearers
Verify SN starts data forwarding to MN
Verify SN sends Secondary RAT Data Usage Report message with correct data volume info
If applicable, verify path update procedure initiation
Send UE Context Release message from MN; confirm SN releases radio and C-plane resources
Verify any ongoing data forwarding continues after resource release
expectedResults: SgNB Release Request Acknowledge message is received confirming SN Release
UE releases SCG configuration successfully or handles failure as per spec
SN Status Transfer message is sent for applicable bearers
Data forwarding starts without interruption
Secondary RAT Data Usage Report message accurately reflects data volumes
Path update procedure is initiated if applicable
UE Context Release triggers resource release at SN
No service gaps or errors during procedure
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Monitor signalling messages between MN and SN
Check UE state and response to RRCConnectionReconfiguration
Inspect SN logs for SN Status Transfer and data forwarding start
Validate Secondary RAT Data Usage Report contents
Verify resource release confirmation on SN

title: Negative Test: SN Rejects MN Initiated SN Release Request Due to SN Change Procedure
description: Validate that SN rejects the MN initiated SN Release request when a SN change procedure is triggered by SN, and MN handles the rejection properly.
preconditions: MN and SN connected with UE in EN-DC mode
SN change procedure is triggered at SN during MN initiated SN Release
testSteps: Access 5G management interface and authenticate
MN sends SgNB Release Request message to SN
SN rejects the SN Release request due to ongoing SN change procedure
MN receives rejection and does not proceed with RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to UE
Verify MN cancels any data forwarding address setup
Verify MN handles rejection gracefully without service disruption
expectedResults: SN sends rejection response to MN
MN receives rejection and cancels SN Release procedure
No RRCConnectionReconfiguration message is sent to UE for SCG release
No unintended service interruption or failure occurs
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Capture signalling to verify rejection message from SN
Monitor MN handling of rejection and cancellation of forwarding addresses
Confirm no RRC signalling towards UE for SCG release

title: Edge Case: UE Unable to Fully Comply with RRCConnectionReconfiguration Message During MN Initiated SN Release
description: Test behavior when UE cannot fully comply with the SCG release configuration included in RRCConnectionReconfiguration message during MN initiated SN Release.
preconditions: MN and SN connected with UE active
UE has limited capability to comply with SCG release configuration
testSteps: MN sends SgNB Release Request to SN
SN confirms SN Release
MN sends RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to UE indicating SCG configuration release
UE fails to comply with part or all of the configuration
UE triggers reconfiguration failure procedure
Verify MN and SN handle the failure procedure appropriately
expectedResults: UE initiates reconfiguration failure procedure as per spec
MN and SN maintain stable state without unintended resource release
Data forwarding and connectivity remain intact or recovery procedures are triggered
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Monitor UE response to RRCConnectionReconfiguration
Check signalling messages for reconfiguration failure procedure
Verify system stability and data forwarding continuity

title: Positive Test: SN Initiated Secondary Node Release Procedure Successful Completion
description: Verify that the SN can successfully initiate the SN Release procedure, MN confirms release, and proper UE context release and data forwarding occur.
preconditions: SN and MN connected with UE context in EN-DC mode
Data forwarding addresses are configured if applicable
testSteps: SN sends SgNB Release Required message to MN, optionally with inter-node delta configuration message
MN responds with SgNB Release Confirm message including data forwarding addresses if applicable
SN starts data forwarding and stops user data delivery to UE
MN sends RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to UE to release entire SCG configuration
UE applies configuration or triggers reconfiguration failure procedure if unable
SN sends SN Status Transfer message for RLC AM bearers
SN starts data forwarding to MN
SN sends Secondary RAT Data Usage Report with data volume information
If applicable, path update procedure is initiated
MN sends UE Context Release message; SN releases radio and C-plane resources
Verify any ongoing data forwarding continues after resource release
expectedResults: MN acknowledges SN Release with SgNB Release Confirm
UE releases SCG configuration or handles failure properly
SN Status Transfer message is sent correctly
Data forwarding starts and continues seamlessly
Secondary RAT Data Usage Report reflects accurate data volumes
Path update procedure is initiated if applicable
SN releases UE context resources without interrupting ongoing data forwarding
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Monitor signalling messages between SN and MN
Verify UE state changes and reconfiguration response
Check SN logs for SN Status Transfer and data forwarding
Validate Secondary RAT Data Usage Report contents
Confirm resource release on SN after UE Context Release

title: Negative Test: MN Rejects SN Initiated SN Release Due to Internal Conditions
description: Test that MN can reject SN Initiated SN Release request if internal conditions such as ongoing SN change procedure prevent release.
preconditions: SN initiates SN Release procedure
MN has ongoing SN change procedure or similar preventing release
testSteps: SN sends SgNB Release Required message to MN
MN rejects the release request due to ongoing SN change procedure
Verify SN handles rejection properly and does not proceed with release
Ensure no RRCConnectionReconfiguration message is sent to UE
Confirm user data delivery continues uninterrupted
expectedResults: MN sends rejection response to SN
SN cancels or defers SN Release procedure
No SCG release signalling towards UE
No disruption to user data or ongoing sessions
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Monitor signalling for rejection from MN to SN
Verify SN does not proceed with release
Confirm UE continues data reception without SCG release

title: Edge Case: UE Fails to Fully Comply with RRCConnectionReconfiguration in SN Initiated SN Release
description: Verify system behavior when UE cannot fully comply with SCG release configuration in RRCConnectionReconfiguration message during SN initiated SN Release.
preconditions: SN initiates SN Release
UE has limited capability to comply with SCG release
testSteps: SN sends SgNB Release Required message to MN
MN sends RRCConnectionReconfiguration message to UE indicating SCG release
UE fails to comply fully and triggers reconfiguration failure procedure
Verify MN and SN handle failure procedure appropriately
Monitor data forwarding and resource management
expectedResults: UE initiates reconfiguration failure as per specification
MN and SN maintain stable state without premature resource release
Data forwarding remains uninterrupted or recovery procedures are triggered
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Monitor UE signalling for reconfiguration failure
Check MN and SN logs for failure handling
Verify data forwarding continuity

title: Performance Test: SN Release Procedure Execution Under High Load Conditions
description: Evaluate the performance and timing of MN and SN initiated SN Release procedures when multiple UEs undergo release concurrently under high load.
preconditions: Network supports EN-DC with multiple active UEs
High traffic load and multiple SN Release procedures triggered simultaneously
testSteps: Simultaneously initiate MN initiated SN Release procedures for multiple UEs
Simultaneously initiate SN initiated SN Release procedures for multiple UEs
Monitor signalling flow timings for SgNB Release Request/Required and Confirm/Acknowledge messages
Measure latency for RRCConnectionReconfiguration and UE Context Release processing
Observe data forwarding coordination and Secondary RAT Data Usage Report message timing
Assess system resource utilization during procedure execution
expectedResults: All SN Release procedures complete successfully within acceptable latency limits
No message loss or misordering occurs
Data forwarding coordination minimizes service gaps
System resources remain within operational thresholds
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Capture and analyze signalling traces for timing and order
Monitor system CPU, memory, and network usage
Verify successful completion of all release procedures

title: Security Test: Authentication and Authorization Checks During SN Release Procedure
description: Verify that only authenticated and authorized MN and SN entities can initiate or confirm SN Release procedures.
preconditions: MN and SN entities have authentication credentials configured
Security policies enforce authorization for SN Release operations
testSteps: Attempt to initiate SN Release procedure from an unauthenticated MN entity
Attempt to confirm SN Release from unauthorized SN entity
Attempt SN Release procedure initiation with invalid credentials or corrupted messages
Validate that system rejects unauthorized requests
Verify that logs record authentication/authorization failures
Confirm no SCG release or UE context release occurs from unauthorized requests
expectedResults: Unauthorized SN Release initiation or confirmation requests are rejected
System logs authentication and authorization failures
No unintended resource release or signalling towards UE occurs
Security policies enforced consistently
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with authorized and unauthorized credentials as needed
verificationMethods: Review rejection messages and error codes
Inspect system security logs for recorded events
Monitor system state to confirm no unauthorized release

title: Integration Test: MN and SN Coordination During SN Release with CPA and Inter-SN CPC Cancellation
description: Test the interaction between MN and SN when SN Release is performed with CPA or inter-SN CPC configured, ensuring proper cancellation of CPAC and CPC configurations.
preconditions: Network configured with CPA and/or inter-SN CPC features
MN and SN have prepared PSCells and CPAC configurations
testSteps: MN initiates SN Release procedure by sending SgNB Release Request
SN confirms with SgNB Release Request Acknowledge
MN cancels all CPC configurations with target candidate SN(s) upon reception
MN cancels all CPAC configurations in candidate SN if applicable
For SN initiated inter-SN CPC, MN triggers Conditional PSCell Change Cancel procedure towards source SN
Verify proper signalling exchange for cancellation procedures
Verify that prepared PSCells at candidate SN are cancelled
Verify UE context release at SN and data forwarding coordination
expectedResults: MN correctly cancels CPC and CPAC configurations
Conditional PSCell Change Cancel procedure triggered as required
No residual prepared PSCells remain at candidate SN
SN Release completes successfully with proper resource release
No service disruption during cancellation
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate with appropriate credentials
verificationMethods: Monitor signalling messages for CPC and CPAC cancellations
Inspect SN and MN logs for configuration changes
Validate UE context release and data forwarding status

title: Usability Test: Clarity and Simplicity of SN Release Procedure Signalling and Error Handling
description: Evaluate the clarity and user experience of SN Release procedure signalling flows and error indications from the perspective of network operators and UE behavior.
preconditions: Test environment with SN and MN supporting SN Release
Access to signalling logs and UE debug information
testSteps: Simulate MN initiated SN Release with successful completion
Simulate SN initiated SN Release with successful completion
Trigger rejection scenarios from SN and MN respectively
Cause UE to perform reconfiguration failure procedure
Review signalling messages and error codes for clarity
Assess logs and interfaces used by network operators for troubleshooting
Gather UE debug info and user impact reports
Evaluate documentation clarity and operator feedback
expectedResults: Signalling messages and error indications are clear and unambiguous
Operators can easily identify procedure status and failure causes
UE behavior aligns with specification and provides understandable failure procedures
Overall procedure is simple to monitor and troubleshoot
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate
Use monitoring and logging interfaces to capture signalling and error information
verificationMethods: Review signalling message content and error codes
Analyze operator interface usability
Collect UE debug logs and user reports
Conduct operator feedback sessions

title: Compatibility Test: SN Release Procedure Across Different EN-DC Version Implementations
description: Validate that SN Release procedures initiated by MN or SN operate correctly across different EN-DC protocol versions and implementations.
preconditions: Test environment with MN and SN using different EN-DC versions (e.g., release 15, 16, 17)
UE is capable of handling respective versions
testSteps: Perform MN initiated SN Release with MN and SN on different EN-DC versions
Perform SN initiated SN Release with MN and SN on different EN-DC versions
Verify that signalling messages are correctly interpreted and accepted
Ensure UE configuration release and reconfiguration failure procedures execute correctly
Check for any version-related incompatibilities or errors
Test feature-specific scenarios such as CPA and inter-SN CPC cancellation across versions
expectedResults: SN Release procedures complete successfully regardless of version differences
No protocol errors or message rejections due to version incompatibility
UE behavior is consistent and compliant with respective versions
CPA and CPC related features operate correctly across versions
commandsUsed: Access the 5G management interface through the provided connection method and authenticate
Verify version information on MN and SN nodes
verificationMethods: Capture signalling traces and analyze protocol compliance
Inspect UE logs for error messages
Compare behaviour across different version setups
